preamble: The hardening and fronting of the voiced uvular fricative [ʁ] into the voiced velar stop [g] is a documented process in several language families, often occurring as part of systemic shifts in the posterior consonant space.

examples.note: Historical uvular fricatives or stops 'hardened' and fronted into velar stops in certain Gyalrongic varieties.
Proposed fronting of uvular fricatives to velar stops/fricatives in branches like Lycian (Kloekhorst, 2016).

sources: Phonological studies on Tibetic historical sound changes.
